First attested as the phonetic component of 憐 in stone carvings in a form ancestral to the small seal script. In the small seal script, 舛 is graphically detached from the 大 with the dots, which in turn is split vertically and interpreted as the unrelated 炎 ; Shuowen erroneously interprets it as an ideogrammic compound ( 會意 / 会意 ): 炎 ( “ fire; flame ” ) + 舛 ( “ feet ” ) . [ 1 ] In the regular script, the top further corrupts into the unrelated 米 .
